content.problemStatement: In this task, we will create a program that formats an email address by removing unnecessary spaces. This helps practice basic string methods like `.strip()` and `.replace() for cleaning user input.
constraints: Each email line length after trimming newline ≤ 100 characters
Email strings consist of printable ASCII characters and spaces only
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single email address possibly containing space characters anywhere in the string. The line may have leading or trailing spaces.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line in the exact format: "Formatted email: <formatted_email>" where every space character in the original email is replaced by an underscore ('_').

sampleTestCases.output:
Formatted email: john.doe_@example.com
Formatted email: jane_doe123_@gmail.com
sampleTestCases.explanation: Each space in the original email is replaced by an underscore.
No spaces exist, so the email remains unchanged.
Both leading and trailing spaces become underscores.

solution.approach: For each test case read the whole line as a raw string (including possible leading/trailing spaces). Iterate over the characters and build a new string: if the character is a space, append '_' otherwise append the original character. Finally print the required prefix followed by the transformed string. The algorithm is O(L) per test case where L is the length of the line, and uses O(L) auxiliary space for the output string.
solution.timeComplexity: O(total_input_characters) – each character is inspected once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(L) per test case for the transformed string (output only).
